purpose: Regression validation for the docs-consolidator SKILL. Each case checks whether an agent consolidating project documentation preserves durable knowledge, removes stale lifecycle content, repairs references, and respects stop conditions without prescribing a single cleanup method.
format_note: Proposed schema. Evaluate each case by checking the agent's document inventory, canonical-destination choices, merge/delete decisions, reference repair, validation steps, and final report against the must / must_not rubric. The scenario is the triggering situation; the expected fields describe the output the agent should reach.
how_to_run: Human-reviewed or harness-reviewed. Present the scenario as a repo documentation cleanup task, invoke the skill, then inspect the agent's stated canonical documents, retained knowledge, removed files, repaired links, and validation results. A case passes only if every must holds and no must_not holds.

cases.scenario: A repository has README.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, and several old design notes that all describe the same current system boundary and data flow.
must: choose one canonical destination for the durable architecture knowledge
merge the valid current-state content into the canonical document
delete or demote the duplicate notes instead of keeping multiple current-state sources
must_not: leave multiple files claiming the same architectural authority
preserve task-history prose as if it were current architecture

cases.scenario: A duplicate document exists only because it is easier to edit than the canonical file, and it adds no distinct long-term role.
must: prefer removing the duplicate after extracting any durable content
keep only the file with the real canonical responsibility
avoid preserving extra files just for convenience
must_not: keep a redundant file without a distinct role
treat edit convenience as a reason to retain duplication

cases.scenario: Two documentation files overlap: one is a stable entrypoint with a narrow source-of-truth role, the other is a longer note with duplicated prose and a few unique durable facts.
must: choose the file with the clearer current-state role and unique authority as the canonical home
merge the unique durable facts from the other file into the chosen canonical document
delete the file that adds no distinct long-term role
must_not: delete the file with the stronger ownership or entrypoint role
keep both files without assigning a distinct purpose

cases.scenario: A docs cleanup skill is about to modify repository documentation, but the repo has a root AGENTS.md that defines operating rules and scope boundaries.
must: follow the root AGENTS.md as the controlling operating rule before editing
defer to more specific maintained repository rules when they conflict with the skill text
keep the edit plan aligned with the repository's current-state workflow
must_not: treat the skill text as overriding the root AGENTS.md
ignore repository-specific operating rules during cleanup

cases.scenario: The repo contains docs/ACTIVE.md, a completed implementation plan, and a memory/inbox tree full of dated work logs.
must: extract only durable constraints, decisions, and validation knowledge
merge durable knowledge into canonical docs
remove the completed plan and the lifecycle tree if no external requirement justifies keeping them
must_not: keep completed plans as maintained documentation
move stale content into a new archive directory

cases.scenario: A root entry document is growing large because it mixes canonical knowledge with task history and multiple long examples.
must: keep root entry docs especially small
split or delete historical material instead of expanding the file
avoid broad rewrites unless the canonical role must change
must_not: let a root entry doc grow into a dump of mixed historical material
rewrite the root entry doc broadly when a smaller edit will do

cases.scenario: Several nested AGENTS.md files repeat the same repo-wide rules, while only one subdirectory has a real runtime-specific boundary.
must: keep the local AGENTS.md only where it adds a distinct local boundary or validation rule
move shared rules to the root AGENTS.md
remove redundant copies that only repeat global boilerplate
must_not: keep local AGENTS.md files for symmetry only
delete a file that encodes a genuine local ownership or runtime constraint

cases.scenario: A maintained documentation file has grown beyond a few hundred lines and now mixes durable knowledge with task history.
must: treat the size as a signal to split or remove historical material
prefer moving durable content into smaller canonical docs
avoid expanding the large file further unless the role requires it
must_not: keep growing a large mixed-purpose file by default
use file size as a reason to retain history

cases.scenario: A repository has a CLAUDE.md that mostly links to canonical docs and serves as the documented entrypoint for contributors and agents.
must: treat the file as a purposeful entrypoint or navigation index rather than automatic duplication
keep the file when it identifies scope and points directly to canonical documents
avoid deleting it solely because it contains links
must_not: delete a CLAUDE.md that has a distinct navigation or ownership role
equate link-heavy structure with redundancy

cases.scenario: The cleanup deletes several docs and rewrites canonical targets, so some links and doc references may be stale.
must: search for removed paths and filenames
repair valid references across docs, code, configs, scripts, and comments
repair direct root links to canonical targets
run git diff --check and report the result
must_not: leave broken links behind
claim completion without reporting validation results

cases.scenario: A deleted documentation path is still embedded in a script, config file, or code comment.
must: scan the repository for the deleted path beyond markdown links
update every surviving hard-coded reference that still matters
report any remaining unresolved references explicitly
must_not: repair only markdown links
leave stale hard-coded paths in code or config

cases.scenario: A retained document still points to a deleted path for historical compatibility, but that path is not required by any external contract.
must: treat compatibility references as intentional only when they are explicitly justified
keep compatibility references narrow and documented
remove historical noise that is not required by an external contract
must_not: preserve compatibility references by default
leave broad historical path references in place without a reason

cases.scenario: A document may be a security policy, compliance record, release note with external obligations, or the only evidence of an unclear runtime boundary.
must: leave the file unchanged when repository evidence is insufficient
report the uncertainty instead of guessing
limit unresolved items to the smallest possible set
must_not: delete protected content without strong evidence
force a canonical choice when the repository does not support it

cases.scenario: The repo has tracked generated logs, crash output, or build artifacts that look like documentation.
must: treat generated artifacts as non-maintained documentation
keep any genuine reusable finding elsewhere before removing the generated output
avoid creating a new archive tree for the removed material
must_not: preserve generated output as if it were canonical knowledge
delete fixtures or golden files that are intentionally tracked
